properties.text: What is Elon Musk saying about Twitter recently?
properties.investigation_goal: What is Elon Musk saying about Twitter recently?

properties.text: Strategic question: Targeted, latest-only search for tweets Elon Musk authored that explicitly mention 'Twitter' (case-insensitive). Excluding replies focuses on original statements and reduces noise from conversational replies.

Strategic question: Retrieve Elon Musk's recent chronological posts to capture any mentions of Twitter that don't include the word 'Twitter' (e.g., replies, follow-ups, linked threads), and to provide the timeline context for any tweets found by the targeted search.

Strategic question: Gather trending topics in the United States to determine whether Twitter or related themes (free speech, moderation, platform changes) are prominent in the public conversation — useful for interpreting why Musk might be commenting now and for prioritizing which tweets to investigate further.

properties.content: Convert an image to a video with one tap when posting!

source_info.follow_up_needed: Request/inspect the linked announcement or release notes: which platforms (iOS/Android/web), rollout timeline, and demo.

properties.content: Grok Code is now seeing more usage on OpenRouter than all others combined https://t.co/iKSmsuefq6

source_info.follow_up_needed: Request metrics/source backing this usage claim (API logs, OpenRouter stats), timeframe, and definition of 'all others combined'.

properties.content: Updated Grok and 𝕏 app now available for download

app: app (possibly 'X' app or Musk-related app; unicode character ambiguous)

source_info.follow_up_needed: Clarify which app is referenced by the unicode character, request download links, changelog, and platform availability.

properties.content: RT @dvorahfr: Have you noticed that you can access Grok from the banner. https://t.co/b2uwM60zc4

source_info.follow_up_needed: Confirm whether Elon Musk retweeted or posted this, capture the screenshot/URL of the banner, determine rollout scope (global vs limited), and check any related official announcement from X/Twitter.

properties.text: Strategic question: Direct, targeted latest-only search for tweets authored by Elon Musk that explicitly mention the word 'Twitter' (case-insensitive). This addresses the highest-priority gap: any direct statements that name the platform.

Strategic question: Catches statements about Twitter-related topics where he may avoid the brand name (e.g., 'the platform', 'X', 'free speech', verification, subscription). Complements the strict 'Twitter' search to reduce false negatives.

parameters.query: from:elonmusk ("the platform" OR "the company" OR X OR "free speech" OR moderation OR verification OR subscription OR "Twitter Blue" OR policy)

properties.text: Strategic question: Fetch Elon Musk's recent chronological posts (including replies and threads). This provides context and yields tweet IDs and timestamps necessary for conversation-level analysis (tweet_thread.php or latest_replies.php) in follow-up steps.

Strategic question: Retrieve profile metadata (display name, bio, rest_id, follower counts). The rest_id and profile info let us validate the account and supply IDs to other endpoints (tweet.php, tweet_thread.php) for deeper investigation.

Strategic question: See who Elon follows (e.g., @Twitter, @TwitterSupport, journalists, key accounts). This helps identify related accounts and possible conversational partners or official channels he might cite when discussing the platform.

source_info.follow_up_needed: Open the linked media to confirm what was animated and whether this references a new Grok feature or demo.

properties.text: Strategic question: One targeted, latest-only search limited to Elon Musk that covers explicit mentions of 'Twitter' plus common alternative phrases he uses when talking about the product/policies. Using a single multi-term query avoids multiple search.php calls while minimizing false negatives.

parameters.query: from:elonmusk (Twitter OR twitter OR "the platform" OR X OR "free speech" OR verification OR subscribe)

properties.text: Strategic question: Fetch Elon Musk's recent chronological posts (original tweets and tweets that are replies) to find mentions of the platform that do not use the word 'Twitter' or that appear as part of longer threads. This provides timeline context and helps identify tweet IDs for deeper conversation analysis.

Strategic question: Verify the current account handle/profile metadata (display name, whether the account has been renamed or suspended, follower counts). This helps explain prior failed searches (e.g., if handle changed) and confirms we are querying the right account.

Strategic question: Determine whether Twitter-related topics (Twitter, X, free speech, moderation, verification) are currently trending in the U.S. Knowing public salience helps prioritize which of Musk's posts are likely to be reactions to events and guides follow-up (which tweets to thread/analyze).
